title: Evangelion in Context
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion in Context             Quote Post by drinian » Sun Sep 25, 2005 2:19 pm I came across this older article recently. In terms of topic, it's all over the place, but talks about Anno's intellectual influences, changing the plot of Eva in response to the Aum Shirynko (spelling?) sarin attacks, and the phenomenon of Rei as a new type of female anime character, among other things. [url]http://www.nettime.org/Lists-Archives/nettime-l-9802/msg00101.html[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Sep 25, 2005 10:40 pm Azuma Hiroki wrote: But anyway, the original scenario is so shockingly close to the political motivation of the Aum Shinrikyo group, they fight against the upshot of the enemy, without knowing what the enemy really is.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sun Sep 25, 2005 11:58 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: This is interesting but seems awfully simplistic. Ko-girls and Otaku? Japan isn't all that different is it? I'm finding it hard to believe that most young teenagers are either reclusive geeks or part time prositiutes?
